articles.title: Ebola outbreak: WHO declares global health emergency in Congo, Uganda
articles.description: ABUJA, Nigeria — The World Health Organization declared the Ebola disease outbreak caused by a rare virus in Congo and neighboring Uganda a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday, after more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths. WHO said the outbreak doesn’t meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency like COVID-19, and [...]... Keep on reading: Ebola outbreak: WHO declares global health emergency in Congo, Uganda

articles.title: ITCZ to bring rains over Southern Mindanao on Monday – Pagasa
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Intertropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) is expected to bring cloudy skies and scattered rains over southern Mindanao on Monday, May 18, according to the state weather bureau. The ITCZ, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa), refers to the cloud clusters that make up a line where winds from the northern and southern hemispheres meet. READ: Agriculture officials fear ‘super’ El Niño In its morning weather forecast, Pagasa weather specialist Aldczar Aurelio said the ITCZ will cause scattered rains and thunderstorms over the following areas: “We expect this ITCZ to continue its impact [...]... Keep on reading: ITCZ to bring rains over Southern Mindanao on Monday – Pagasa

articles.title: Severe storms possible Sunday night near Kansas City; Monday could be worse
articles.description: Strong to severe storms are possible Sunday evening and overnight in the Kansas City area. The Storm Prediction Center, part of the National Weather Service, has placed parts of far northeast Kansas and northwest Missouri at an enhanced risk (Level 3 of 5) of severe weather. Meanwhile, areas just north of Kansas City are at a slight risk (Level 2 of 5), while the metro area is at a marginal risk (Level 1 of 5).

articles.title: Longwood Gardens: A jewel of nature
articles.description: After industrialist Pierre S. du Pont bought a small farm outside Philadelphia to save its trees from being sold for lumber, he spent the rest of his life creating his dream oasis. Today, the 1,700-acre Longwood Gardens, recently expanded, continues its mission of providing "joy and inspiration to everyone through the beauty of nature, conservation, and learning." Jim Axelrod reports.
